---
title: "Scanning Additional Documents"
---

---
title: "Scanning Additional Documents"
source: "https://support.hyland.com/r/Current/Hyland-Capture/tpc1646248501163"
section: "Hyland Capture"
---

When you have a pending batch that was captured using a scan input source in the document review layout, you can use any existing scan input source to scan additional documents into the batch under the same Content Queue.

Note: If any fields have been configured for bar code extraction and assigned to document classes in the batch's assigned Content Queue, bar code extraction is performed on any newly scanned documents, but it is not performed again on any previously captured documents in the batch.
To scan additional documents into a pending batch:

1.  Open the Hyland Capture application and log in (see Opening the Hyland Capture Application). The Hyland Capture Scan and Import interface is displayed, showing one of the following layouts:
-
If there are no pending batches, the document capture layout is displayed.

-
If there is a pending batch, the document review layout is displayed.

Note:
If you are already logged in to the Hyland Capture application and using the Verify Data interface, you must switch to the Scan and Import interface (see Switching the Interface).

2.  If there are no pending batches, capture documents using a scan input source to create a new batch (see Capturing Documents). The document review layout is displayed.
3.  Above the thumbnail viewer on the right side of the layout, click the Scan More Pages icon.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
The Capture Toolbar is displayed, showing the batch's assigned Content Queue on the left.

4.  To automatically apply the same document class to each new document you scan into the batch, select a document class from the classification drop-down list.
5.  Select a scan input source from the Input Source drop-down list.
6.  Click Start Capture. The new documents are captured into the system and added to the end of the batch.  Tip:
To ensure the newly captured documents are properly prepared for verification, organize or reorganize the batch documents once the new documents have been added (see Organizing Captured Documents).
